headline: Pan Borneo Highway 60% Complete After 9 Years
context: The Pan Borneo Highway launched in 2015 reached only 60% completion by early 2026 despite RM35 billion in allocated spending.

cards.big: Pan Borneo Highway only 60% done after RM35 billion spent
cards.sub: Original 2024 completion deadline pushed to 2028 at the earliest

cards.big: Sarawak section is 72% complete while Sabah lags at 48%
cards.sub: Contractor disputes and land acquisition delays slowed Sabah 706km stretch

cards.big: Cost overruns total RM8.3 billion above the 2016 revised budget
cards.sub: PAC report found 12 change orders worth RM2.1 billion lacked proper approval

cards.big: Only 340km of planned 2,325km is fully open to four-lane traffic
cards.sub: Communities along incomplete sections still rely on dangerous single-lane roads

cards.big: Highway delays reveal whether Borneo infrastructure receives equal federal priority
Nine years and 60% completion — Pan Borneo's pace reveals infrastructure as political leverage.
